channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Intellectually disabled teen shot by Idaho police dies after being removed from life support
item.description: BOISE, Idaho (AP) — An autistic, nonverbal teenage boy who was shot repeatedly by Idaho police from the other side of a chain-link fence while he was holding a knife died Saturday after being removed from life support, his family said.
item.dc:creator: MARTHA BELLISLE and REBECCA BOONE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: US won't say whether it's facilitating return of mistakenly deported man, despite judge's order
item.description: The Trump administration confirmed to a federal judge Saturday that a Maryland man who was mistakenly deported last month remains confined in a notorious prison in El Salvador.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Intellectually disabled teen shot by Idaho police dies after being removed from life support
item.description: Victor Perez — an autistic and nonverbal 17-year-old boy who was shot repeatedly on April 5th by Idaho police — has died after being removed from life support.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump administration says it will exclude some electronics from reciprocal tariffs
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — The Trump administration late Friday said it would exclude electronics like smartphones and laptops from reciprocal tariffs, a move that could help keep the prices down for popular consumer electronics that aren’t usually made in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MAE ANDERSON and MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Popular soup, bowl products may be contaminated; recall issued
item.description: The USDA has issued a nationwide public health alert warning consumers that the products could be contaminated with wood.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Langenfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Regulators order corrective action as Keystone Pipeline operators aim to restore service
item.description: Federal regulators have ordered the operator of the Keystone Pipeline to take several corrective actions after a rupture caused 147,000 gallons of oil to spill onto farmland in North Dakota.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Judge relaxes ban on DOGE access to sensitive US Treasury information
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A New York judge has relaxed a ban she’d put on Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency preventing it from accessing sensitive Treasury Department information related to millions of Americans. Judge Jeannette A.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Kansas man wrongly accused in Chiefs parade shooting found dead
item.description: An Olathe, Kansas man who was wrongly accused of being a "shooter" and "illegal alien" at the Chiefs Super Bowl rally in 2024 has died.
item.dc:creator: Brian Dulle, KREX 5

item.title: Kansas man who was falsely accused of being a shooter after Chiefs’ 2024 Super Bowl win has died
item.description: A Kansas man who was falsely accused of being among the shooters who opened fire at a rally celebrating the Kansas City Chiefs’ Super Bowl victory last year has died.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump administration ordered to unfreeze funding in dispute with Maine over transgender students
item.description: PORTLAND, Maine (AP) — A federal judge has ordered the Trump administration to unfreeze funds intended for a Maine child nutrition program that were suspended amid a disagreement between the state and the president over transgender athletes.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Lawyers for Sean 'Diddy' Combs want jurors at trial questioned about sex, drugs and violence
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Lawyers for Sean “Diddy” Combs are urging a judge to let prospective jurors at the hip-hop mogul's upcoming sex trafficking trial be questioned about their views regarding sex, drugs and violence.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 5 major unreleased movies we'll (probably) never get to see
item.description: "Coyote vs. Acme" was recently rescued from obscurity after being shelved by Warner Bros. Discovery. But other films haven't been so lucky.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KRON4

item.title: Trump exempts phones, computers, other electronics from reciprocal tariffs
item.description: The Trump administration is exempting electronics such as phones, computers and other electronics from the "reciprocal" tariffs imposed on other nations, including China.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Former LAPD officer pleads guilty to murder in DUI crash that killed family of 3
item.description: A former Los Angeles police officer was off-duty and speeding at 150 mph when he crashed into two vehicles on Sept. 26, 2017.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, NewsNation

item.title: An emboldened anti-abortion faction wants women who have abortions to face criminal charges
item.description: Many people involved in the abortion debate say a movement of so-called abortion abolitionists who want to punish women for having abortions is widening its influence.
item.dc:creator: Christine Fernando, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Democrats dislike the 'chaos' of Trump's trade war but are OK with some tariffs
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Democrats are quick to say that President Donald Trump’s tariffs are horrible, awful, terrible. But Democrats are also stressing that they are not inherently anti-tariff. What Trump's political opponents say they really dislike is the “chaos” he has unleashed.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK and MATT BROWN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Take a trip to Ohio to learn about William McKinley, Trump's much-admired Gilded Age president
item.description: If there’s one place where interest in William McKinley has remained unwavering, it's in the 25th president's native northeast Ohio.
item.dc:creator: Julie Carr Smyth, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Columbia University activist Mahmoud Khalil can be deported, immigration judge rules
item.description: JENA, La. (AP) — Columbia University graduate student Mahmoud Khalil can be forced out of the country as a national security risk, an immigration judge in Louisiana ruled Friday after lawyers argued the legality of deporting the activist who participated in pro-Palestinian demonstrations.
item.dc:creator: SARA CLINE and KATE BRUMBACK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Divers search for parts after NYC helicopter crash killed a family of five and the pilot, a Navy vet
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Divers using sonar searched Friday for key pieces of a sightseeing helicopter that broke apart in midair and plunged into the Hudson River between Manhattan and New Jersey. All six people aboard were killed — a family of five from Spain and the pilot, a 36-year-old U.S. Navy veteran.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, DAVE COLLINS and TED SHAFFREY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Rachel Morin murder: Illegal immigrant suspect denied ever being in Maryland at time of crime
item.description: The murder trial of Victor Antonio Martinez-Hernandez, the Salvadoran illegal immigrant accused of murdering Bel-Air woman Rachel Morin in August 2023, will continue Monday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US may already be in recession: BlackRock CEO
item.description: BlackRock CEO Larry Fink said during a recent interview that the United States may already be in a recession or getting very close to one as a result of President Trump’s sweeping tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump eyes major cuts to NOAA research
item.description: The Trump administration is eyeing cuts to climate, weather and ocean research in a draft budget blueprint for the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA).
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Fox News True Crime Newsletter: Kohberger's evidence fight, Vallow's latest trial, Read's jury selection
item.description: Stay up to date with the Fox News True Crime Newsletter, which brings you the latest cases ripped from the headlines, from crime to courts, legal and scandal.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Feds say agents went to LA schools to do welfare check on migrant children
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Homeland Security officers visited two Los Angeles public elementary schools this week to do a welfare check on migrant children, not for immigration enforcement, the department said Friday. The officers were denied access by both principals.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Tufts student from Turkey details arrest, crowded detention conditions in new court filing
item.description: A Tufts University doctoral student from Turkey is demanding her release after she was detained by immigration officials near her Massachusetts home, detailing how she was scared when the men grabbed her phone and feared she would be killed.
item.dc:creator: KATHY McCORMACK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Immigration judge allows Mahmoud Khalil deportation effort to proceed
item.description: A judge in former Columbia University activist Mahmoud Khalil's case ruled against him Friday, finding he is eligible for deportation while giving his legal team a little less than two weeks to respond.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Amazon CEO: Sellers will try to pass tariff costs along to buyers
item.description: Amazon’s network includes millions of third-party sellers who are either in China or whose products originate from there.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KRON4

item.title: Greenland space base commander removed after Vance criticism
item.description: The Trump administration removed the Greenland space base commander after learning she sent an email with a slight against the vice president.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5

item.title: Plane crash near Florida highway kills 3 on board, injures 1 on the ground
item.description: A spokesperson for the Federal Aviation Administration said the plane, a Cessna 310, crashed at around 10:20 a.m. after departing from the nearby airport.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: Family from Spain, pilot killed in NYC sightseeing helicopter crash
item.description: Photos posted on the helicopter company’s website showed the couple and their children smiling as they boarded just before the flight took off.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Euzarraga, Fox 5

item.title: Report: Drug lord linked to DEA agent’s death freed
item.description: One of the three leaders of the Guadalajara cartel linked to the torture-slaying of Drug Enforcement Administration Agent Enrique “Kiki” Camarena in 1985 has been released in Mexico, according to news reports.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, NewsNation

item.title: A Los Angeles woman arrested in Russia has been freed in a prisoner swap. Here's what we know
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Moscow has freed a Russian American convicted of treason in exchange for a Russian German man jailed on smuggling charges in the U.S. The prisoner swap was completed Thursday and Ksenia Karelina was "on a plane back home to the United States,” U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Republican bill requiring proof of citizenship for voting passes US House
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The House passed one of Republicans' signature issues for the year on Thursday, approving legislation to require proof of U.S. citizenship when registering to vote for federal elections, one of President Donald Trump's top election-related priorities.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINA A. CASSIDY, CHRISTINE FERNANDO and LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: How to view the pink moon and Lyrid meteor shower
item.description: People in the U.S. can expect a few celestial treats in the coming weekends, including a full moon and a meteor shower.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, Fox 5

item.title: House Republicans pass bill requiring proof of citizenship to vote
item.description: House Republicans for a second time passed a stand-alone bill that would require proof of citizenship to vote in federal elections and impose voter roll purge requirements on states. The legislation — formally titled the Safeguard American Voter Eligibility (SAVE) Act — passed in a 220-208 vote.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, ABC 4

item.title: Sweepstakes giant Publishers Clearing House files for bankruptcy
item.description: Publishers Clearing House, famous for its big checks and sweepstakes, files for Chapter 11 bankruptcy as it shifts to digital advertising.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Local businesses react to Trump's tariffs, prepare for potential long-term effects
item.description: Local businesses are feeling anxiety and uncertainty around President Donald Trump’s tariffs — despite the president backing down on his tariffs on most nations for 90 days today.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan May, ABC 4
